item.title: Fortnite: Complete Guide
item.description:
This guide will tell players everything they need to know about Fortnite Battle Royale, Creative Mode, LEGO Fortnite, Fortnite Festival, and Rocket Racing.

  • General Fortnite Guides

    There is a lot to learn about Fortnite , and this section of the article will include all sorts of guides on the most basic as well as more advanced concepts in Fortnite .

    • How-to Guides

      • How to Gift Skins
      • How to Redeem V-Bucks Cards
      • How to Play in Split Screen Mode (Couch Co-Op Guide)
      • How to Play Fortnite Geoguessr
      • How to Play Save the World (& Is It Worth It?)
      • How to Play Creative 2.0
      • How to Set Preferred Item Slots
      • How to Use the Quick Weapon Setting (Item Wheel)
      • How to Farm Gold Fast
      • How to Earn Survivor Medals (& How They Work)
      • How To Level Up Fast
      • How to Mark Enemies
      • How To Mark Opponent With the Flare Gun
      • How To Play OG Map
      • How to Play LEGO Creative Islands
      • How to Find Loot Islands
      • How to Find Combat Caches
      • How To Claim Locations (& What It's For)
      • How to Figure Out If You Are Eligible for the Fortnite FTC Refund
      • How to Get Settlement Money (Fortnite Lawsuit 2022)
      • How to Slide Kick
      • How to Redeem Fortnite Gift Cards
      • How to See Footsteps
      • How To Score Trick Points On a Motorcycle
      • How to Turn on Aim Assist
      • How to Voice Report
      • How to Play on Steam Deck
      • How to Join a Fortnite Tournament
      • How to Voice Report
      • How to Play on Steam Deck
      • How to Restart Fortnite
      • How to See Ping
      • How to Fix 'You Do Not Have Permission to Play' Error
      • How to Jam
      • How to Hit Opponents with a Melee Weapon
      • How to Toggle Confrontational Emotes
      • How to Get a Hired Follower to Eliminate an Opponent
      • How to Easily Hit an Opponent After Jumping Out of a Hiding Place
      • How to Play Fall Guys in Fortnite
      • How to Easily Get a Double Elimination
      • How to Purchase Items Remotely
      • How to Open Collections to View Accolades
      • How to Play Fortnite on iPhone and Android
      • How to Use Underworld Dash
      • How to Toggle Weapon Canting
      • How To Wall Kick & Wall Scramble
      • How to Ledge Jump & Roll Land
      • How to Get a Custom Crosshair
      • How To Play Fortnite xCloud With Decky Loader
      • How To Get Stretched Resolution
      • How to Get the Free Mr Dappermint Skin
      • How to Enable Auto-Fire
      • How to Change Your Default Skin
      • How To Fix Known Issues With Graphics Driver
      • How to Fix Matchmaking Error #2
      • How to Turn Off Simple Edit
      • How to Fix Matchmaking Error #1

    • Beginner Guides

      • Best Controller Settings
      • Tips and Tricks to Victory
      • All Structures to Build in Build Modes (And How to Use Them)
      • Top Building Tips And Tricks
      • Best Building Techniques to Master
      • Top Building Tips And Tricks
      • Tips To Improve Aim in the Battle Royale
      • Best Stretched Resolution for Fortnite
      • Unstable Weapons, Explained

    • FAQ

      • Rocket Racing Explained
      • Fortnite Festival Explained
      • Lego Fortnite Explained
      • When Does the Item Shop Change?
      • Hot Spots Explained
      • Age Rating Restrictions Explained
      • All Weapon Rarities Explained
      • How to Turn on Aim Assist
      • What Is a Creator Made Island?
      • Can You Gift the Fortnite Festival Pass?
      • What Are Ranged Weapons?
      • Who Is The Wanderer?
      • What Is Hitscan in Fortnite?
      • What is Siphon in Fortnite?
      • What Is Sleep Mode in Fortnite?
      • Reboot Cards, Explained
      • Fortnite Blitz Royale Explained
      • LEGO Fortnite Expeditions Explained
      • Reload Map Rotation Schedule

    • Lists

      • Things Only Veterans Remember
      • Forgotten Things That Got Vaulted
      • Best Limited-Time Modes, Ranked
      • Best Guns in Fortnite History, Ranked
      • Every Season Of Fortnite, Ranked
      • Best Fortnite Streamers
      • Best Live Events, Ranked
      • Important Moments In The Storyline
      • Awesome Collabs In Fortnite
      • Most OP Items in Fortnite History
      • Hardest Accolades, Ranked
      • All Battle Royale Maps, Ranked
      • Best Marvel Collabs
      • Best Weapons In Fortnite History, Ranked

  • Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2


    Fortnite Chapter 7 Season 2 introduced players to tons of cosmetics, weapons, and map locations. Gamers have plenty to do this season, and they can learn all about it in this section.

    • Battle Pass & Cosmetics

      • Chapter 7 Season 2 Battle Pass Skins

    • Quests & Events

      • Should You Pick Team Foundation or Ice King?

    • Map

      • Chapter 7 Season 2 NPC Locations

    • Weapons & Items

    • Fortnite Reload

      • Things You Need to Know About Fortnite Reload
      • All Weapons in Fortnite Reload
      • Best Landing Locations
      • Tips and Tricks To Win
      • Quests & Rewards
      • Where to Find the Mythic Goldfish

    • Fortnite OG

      • How to Double Pump
      • Things That Will Make Players Feel Nostalgic
      • All Risky Reels Chest Locations

  • Skins & Cosmetics

    There are thousands of cosmetic items in Fortnite , and this section of the article will show players how they can acquire some of the most coveted skins as well as some cosmetic items that came as a result of collaborations with other video games, franchises, and brands.

    • How-to Guides

      • How to Get Zadie (& All Additional Styles)
      • How to Get Caper and Alias (& Additional Gear)
      • How to Get the Transformers Pack
      • How to Get the Infinite Drift Pack
      • How To Get Eminem Skin
      • How to Get Lewis Hamilton Skins
      • How to Get Invincible Skins (Omni-Man, Invincible, Atom Eve)

  • Fortnite Crew

    Fortnite has its own monthly subscription called the Fortnite Crew. Fans who acquire this pack gain access to a series to unique perks, such as exclusive skins, monthly V-Bucks, and a free Battle Pass.

    • How to Cancel Crew Subscription
    • How To Get The Rocket Pass For Free With Fortnite Crew
    • Is the Opening Night Pass Part of the Fortnite Crew?
    • Winter's Trickster Legacy Set Guide (July 2023)
    • Extraplanar Hunter's Legacy Set Guide (October 2023)
    • Hybrid Evolved’s Legacy Set Guide (November 2023)
    • Grim Reconning's Legacy Set Guide (December 2023)
    • Venomous Legacy Evolved Set Guide (January 2024)
    • Llana Origins Legacy Set Guide (February 2024)
    • Best Fortnite Crew Skins So Far, Ranked
    • Fortnite Crew Skin Tier List

  • Creative Mode

    Creative mode allows players to make their own Fortnite islands and creator experiences. On top of this, they can also play other gamers' maps, practice their combat and building skills, and try out tons of fun minigames to earn XP.

    • Best Tycoon Maps (2025)
    • How to Win in Gemstone Tycoon
    • Best Guess the Song Maps Codes
    • Best Party Game Maps (And The Codes)
    • Best Survival Maps (And The Codes)
    • Best Prop Hunt Maps (And The Codes)

    • Best Escape Rooms
    • How to Play Chained Together in Fortnite
    • How to Enable Proximity Chat
    • Fun Creative Maps to Farm XP
    • All Steal the Brainrot Codes
    • All Don’t Steal My Brainrot Codes

  • LEGO Fortnite Odyssey

    Fortnite joined forces with LEGO to create a survival game mode set in the LEGO world. This is a completely different game set in Fortnite , and fans will learn an entire set of different skills and understand its mechanics in order to play it. This section will tell gamers everything they need to know to play LEGO Fortnite Odyssey .

    • FAQ

      • How Big Is the Lego Fortnite Map?
      • 'Can't Select World' Error Explained

  • Rocket Racing


    Fortnite added Rocket Racing as a permanent game mode. Players who enjoy the racing genre should definitely give this game a try. This section of the article will tell fans everything they need to know.

    • How-to Guides

item.title: How to Unlock Cosmetics and Vehicles in REPO
item.description:
Semiwork has finally released v0.4.0, the Cosmetic Update . While a popular mod has been used for months to add cosmetics to the bots, there are now over 500 cosmetics available as part of the vanilla experience to REPO !

Cosmetics aren't just obtained through any means, and we'll showcase how you can get them below, along with information on the newest vehicles that have hit the tracks in REPO for this exciting major update, and beyond.

How to Unlock Cosmetics in REPO

  • Cosmetics can now be found within Cosmetic Boxes, which are items that spawn randomly during levels .

When you find a Cosmetic Box, you can note its rarity condition based on the color and size of the box itself:

Size

Color

Rarity

Small

Green

Common

Medium

Blue

Uncommon

Large

Purple

Rare

Extra Large

Orange

Epic

To actually unlock the Cosmetic Box, you need to extract it , the same way you'd extract regular loot. However, it's worth noting that these Cosmetic Boxes can be destroyed, as they have a durability bar. However, this health bar can be healed by holding the hand icon to transfer your own health .

If you've managed to safely extract the Cosmetic Box, you will be rewarded with Tax Tokens . The amount of Tax Tokens you receive is dependent on the rarity of the box itself.

Once you return to the Shop, you can interact with the Taxman Machine (shown above) to exchange your Tax Tokens for a random cosmetic. The screen will display the randomly selected cosmetic that you unlock . All players will get a Tax Token, so no player will receive the same cosmetic.

Once you've unlocked a cosmetic, it's permanently unlocked, so you won't have to worry about losing it when you start a new run.

How to Equip and Save Cosmetics in REPO

Viewing your cosmetics in REPO works the same as changing your color previous to the update. Once you've unlocked your first cosmetic, there will be a Customize option on the main menu .

Before starting or joining a lobby, you can now equip your fancy new Head, Body, Arms, or Legs. Once you've made a beautiful Semibot ready for work, you can select "Presets" at the top of the screen to save your outfit .

How to Get Vehicles in REPO

In addition to new cosmetics, semiwork also released new vehicles you can use in REPO . Your Semibots can now cruise around the maps and drive away from monsters. These new vehicles can be purchased from the Shop:

  • Hauler (38-48K)
  • Scouter (18-20K)

The vehicles are used to more quickly scoot around the maps, but they are hard to use and rather fragile, and bumping them into things will drain their batteries, so make sure you have recharged crystals on hand!

R.E.P.O.

Follow
Followed
Horror
Roguelike
Action
Stealth
Systems
Released
February 26, 2025
Developer(s)
semiwork
Publisher(s)
semiwork
Engine
Unity
Multiplayer
Online Co-Op
6 Images

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

Genre(s)
Horror, Roguelike, Action, Stealth
Powered by

item.title: Mixtape Front to Back Achievement/Trophy Guide
item.description:
Front to Back might be one of the more frustrating achievements in Mixtape that could force you to replay the game from the very beginning if you don’t pay enough attention to your surroundings in the first playthrough.

Front to Back requires the player to inspect every interactable object in the bedrooms of the four characters in the story, but even if you miss one item, some players have reported that they can't go back through the chapter selection screen to pick that up. We've got the full list of interactables you need to find to avoid giving you a headache later on, in case you miss one.

You should interact with all items and engage in all conversations in a single run, as sometimes, going through the chapter select won't work.

All Items & Conversations in Rockford’s Room

The very first house that you will visit earlier in the game is Stacey Rockford’s house. You need to explore the upper floor, which includes Stacey’s room, her sister’s room, and a bathroom in the hallway .

These are the items to focus on once you reach the second-floor hallway:

  • Red radio on top of the drawer.
  • A painting hung from the wall next to the staircase.
  • A picture of Stacey, located on the drawer in front of the painting.
  • A family picture hung from the wall, next to Stacey’s sister’s room.
  • A cloth on the sink inside the bathroom.

It's time to head into Stacey's room to interact with these items:

  • Portishead’s Dummy CD on the bookshelf.
  • Cassette recorder on the same bookshelf.
  • David Shire’s Unused Score CD on the shelf next to the PC.
  • VHS tapes next to the TV, in front of the bed.
  • Keyboard plushie on the bed.
  • Skateboard next to the bed.
  • The turntable on the drawer in front of the window.
  • Speak with Slater.
  • Speak with Cass.
  • Cassettes on the PC desk.

Once you reach this part, a new memory will play. When the cinematic is over, and you are back in the room again, go after the following items:

  • Polaroids on the closet door.
  • Speak with Slater.
  • Speak with Cass.
  • VHS player on top of the TV.

The search will be interrupted by another memory, and then you will be returned to the room again. Now, inspect the following:

  • Empty closet.
  • Speak with Slater.
  • Speak with Cass.
  • Polaroids on the wall, next to the room’s door (you will be tasked with finding the three missing polaroids).
  • A Polaroid on the drawer next to the bed, inside a book.
  • A Polaroid on top of a handful of books on the ground, placed next to the bookshelf.
  • A Polaroid on the closet door.
  • Polaroids on the wall, next to the door (again).

Once you have completed the series of Polaroids on the wall, it’s time to move on to the next memory. So, do the following:

  • Speak with Slater.
  • Speak with Cass.
  • Interact with the box on the chair near the PC.

The next memory will play, and then we will be back in the room for the last time. Inspect the following to wrap up Stacey’s room:

  • Speak with Slater.
  • Speak with Cass.
  • Interact with the bulletin board (then interact with every card pinned on the board).

It’s important to check out every card that is attached to the board, or you will risk not securing the achievement.

All Items & Conversations in Debbie’s Room

After searching Stacey’s room, the door to Debbie’s room will be unlocked . So, head inside the room and inspect the following:

  • The box on the ground, next to Cass.
  • The trophy on top of the blue desk.
  • Speak with Cass.
  • Speak with Slater.
  • The wardrobe near the door.

The wardrobe is locked, but trying to access it will grant you new interactable items:

  • Speak with Slater.
  • Speak with Cass.
  • Interact with the picture on top of the green drawer.

This will shoot Stacey to a new memory, where you will get a reason why Jenny Goodspeed is not Stacey’s favorite type, but once she’s back, you get to explore Debbie’s room again:

  • Speak with Slater.
  • Speak with Cass.
  • Open the drawer.
  • Look for the latter piece of the ticket. It’s beneath the stuff inside the drawer, in the top right corner.

When you’re back in the room again, you can pick up the key to the wardrobe. So, do the following:

  • Pick up the seashell on the blue desk.
  • Interact with the wardrobe.

That’s everything you need to inspect in Debbie’s room. This will wrap up the scene in Rockford’s house. Next, you will explore the forests of Blue Moon Lagoon until the story takes you to the next character’s room.

All Items & Conversations in Cassandra’s Room

Several tracks later, you will find yourself in Cassandra’s room . While most of the items are inside the room, there is still one that sits in the hallway. So, we will start with that:

  • Get out of the room and check out Cassandra’s family picture in the hallway. It’s also worth checking out the other room’s door.

The rest of the items are inside Cassandra’s room:

  • The book on the bookshelf next to the entrance door.
  • The bulletin board, next to the bathroom door.
  • The cassette on the drawer next to the bed.
  • Cassandra’s picture hung from the wall, near her desk.
  • A drawing of a flower, hung from the wall, next to the window.
  • Speak with Cass.
  • Speak with Slater.
  • The trophy inside the cabinet.

At this point, you will witness some memories about Cassandra’s past, but you will be back in her room again. So, the search continues:

  • Cassandra’s bed.
  • Speak with Cass.
  • Speak with Slater.
  • The notice on the bathroom wall, next to the mirror.

This will play another memory, before you can resume the inspection:

  • Wallpaper next to the trophy cabinet.
  • Speak with Cass.
  • Speak with Slater.
  • The picture on the tiny table next to the bathroom door.

You will learn something new about Jenny Goodspeed, and then you will be back to Cassandra’s room for more inspection:

  • Speak with Slater.
  • Speak with Cass.
  • Interact with the contract on the bed.

Another break from searching the room. When you are back, there is new stuff to interact with:

  • Trophy cabinet (again).
  • Drapes on top of the window (look up to see the interaction marker).
  • Speak with Cass.
  • Speak with Slater.
  • The open window behind Slater.

This is the final memory from Cassandra. Once you are back, you will engage in two more conversations, and that’s it:

  • Speak with Cass.
  • Speak with Slater.

This wraps up all the events of Cassandra’s room. After you meet with Cassandra’s father, there is only one more room left to explore.

All Items & Conversations in the Ritz (Skippable)

You do not need to interact with any of the items in the Ritz to get the Front to Back achievement. You can read ahead to find the interactables within Slater's room, which are needed. However, we've included the Ritz in case you're curious about what you can find here.

Again, you can totally skip this part, as it won’t affect the progress in Front to Back, but still, here are all the items you can inspect:

  • A poster of Camille on the table.
  • A beer on the box next to the table.
  • An old refrigerator near the railings.
  • A flask on the box next to the yellow car.
  • A radio on a round table.
  • The window of the cabin.
  • Speak with Slater.
  • The color cans on the ground, in front of the cabin.
  • The rocks in front of the sofa.
  • The sofa itself.
  • Speak with Slater (three times).
  • The slingshot on the blue car.

Once you complete the slingshot training memory, you will be done with all the interactions in the Ritz. The next stop is Slater’s room.

All Items & Conversations in Slater’s Room

Later in the game, you will step into Slater’s room . There are many things to inspect here, but you won’t be interrupted by too many memories this time.

Subscribe to the newsletter for essential Mixtape guides

Hungry for more Mixtape achievement help? Subscribing to the newsletter provides thorough, walkthrough-style coverage of Mixtape interactables, checklist-ready guides, and similar game-focused coverage.
By subscribing, you agree to receive newsletter and marketing emails, and accept our Terms of Use and Privacy Policy. You can unsubscribe anytime.

The good news is that everything is located inside the room. So, begin with inspecting the following:

  • The purple electric orb near the staircase.
  • The robot next to the purple orb.
  • The Polaroids near the TV.
  • The book on the sofa.
  • The blue guitar on the sofa.
  • The picture on the drawer next to the window.
  • The vinyl disc on the drawer, near the aquarium.
  • The fish inside the aquarium.
  • The mini-master device on the PC desk.
  • The drawings on the wall above the bed, behind the PC.
  • The purple lava lamp, next to the brown drawer.
  • Speak with Slater.
  • The cassette player next to the lava lamp.

That was a giant list of things to inspect, but that’s almost all of them. Once you are back from listening to Slater’s new song, you will have the following to do:

  • Speak with Slater.
  • Interact with the VHS tape on Slater’s bed.

That wraps up all items to inspect in Slater’s room! If you have done all of the above correctly, you should get a pop-up for securing the Front to Back achievement .

As mentioned earlier, it's safer to inspect all the items and engage in all the conversations in a single run. If you have missed any item or any conversation, try the chapter selection, but it might not get you the achievement if it's not been done in a single run.

Mixtape

Followed

item.title: Roblox Chicken Rocket Codes (May 2026)
item.description:
Chicken Rocket is one of the coolest Roblox games out there, where you strap a super-powered rocket onto a silly chicken and blast it straight up into the endless sky. You’ll zoom higher and higher, snatching up tasty crackers that give you points, dodging exploding bombs that try to knock you off course, and unlocking awesome items like speedy boosters and fancy gliders to go even farther.

With six different rocket types that each have their own special tricks and over a hundred fun gadgets to collect, every launch feels like a new adventure.

Working codes are a total game-changer in Chicken Rocket because they hand you free rewards right away. No waiting around for hours of grinding—you get extra crackers, special boosts, or cool items that make your chicken fly stronger and higher. So if you’re looking for the latest working codes, you’re in the right spot.

Updated May 8, 2026, by Artur Novichenko: At the moment, there are no active Chicken Rocket codes available to redeem in-game. This means you cannot currently claim any bonus rewards such as boosts, items, or currency through codes. The situation may change with future updates or events, where new codes could be added by the developers. It is a good idea to check back regularly, as new freebies can appear at any time and this guide is kept up to date.

There might not be any active codes to redeem right now, but bookmark this page and return later, as we'll add codes right here as soon as they're revealed.

All Chicken Rocket Codes

Are There Working Chicken Rocket Codes?

Right now, there are no active working Chicken Rocket codes, but the developers are always adding new stuff, so things could change any day.

Expired Chicken Rocket Codes

There are also no expired Chicken Rocket codes at this time. The game is still pretty fresh, so the team hasn’t released many yet. We’ll keep watching and add any that pop up!

Codes and the rewards they give are super important for keeping this Roblox game exciting. Imagine getting a free booster that helps your chicken dodge bombs like a pro or extra crackers that fill up your progress bar in seconds. These little surprises let you try out new rockets and gliders without spending forever collecting them yourself.

How to Redeem Chicken Rocket Codes

  • Open Chicken Rocket on Roblox .
  • Roll your chicken over to the Friends and Rewards house and click on it.
  • Type or paste the code into the Enter code box.
  • Hit the redeem button and watch your free rewards appear.

How to Get More Chicken Rocket Codes

The easiest way to stay on top of new codes is to check the game’s official spots. Join the official Chicken Rocket Discord server, where the devs drop hints and sometimes share codes early. And don’t miss the Chicken Rocket Roblox Community group for the latest updates straight from the team.

We update our articles all the time whenever fresh promo codes come out, so popping back here every few days is the smartest move. That way, you’ll always have the newest working codes ready to copy and use for epic chicken launches.

Roblox
Released
September 1, 2006

WHERE TO PLAY

DIGITAL

item.title: Roblox: Build A Boat For Treasure Codes (May 2026)
item.description:
Build A Boat For Treasure has gained popularity on Roblox because of its fantastic concept, where players first have to build a boat and then test it on an obstacle course. It can often be quite challenging for Roblox players to make a good boat in the early stages of the game, which is where the Build A Boat For Treasure codes listed below can come in handy.

Updated April 8, 2026, by Artur Novichenko: A small set of Build A Boat For Treasure codes is currently available, offering a mix of Gold, building materials, and a special Firework reward. These bonuses can help you gather extra resources for construction and add a bit of variety to your builds. While most codes provide simple Gold rewards, others include unique items that can enhance your creations. More codes may be released in future updates, so it is worth checking back regularly for new freebies and changes.

All Codes For Build A Boat For Treasure

Roblox players looking to get some extra gold, blocks, and other building materials for creating a beautiful boat can use the following active codes.

These codes will expire at random intervals, so be sure to redeem them as soon as they become available. Codes can only be redeemed once per account. Be sure to check back frequently, as codes can be released anytime during the month.

Working Build A Boat For Treasure Codes

  • hi - Redeem for 5 Gold
  • Squid Army - Redeem for 22x Ice and 22x Gold
  • =D - Redeem for 5 Gold
  • =P - Redeem for 5 Gold
  • chillthrill709 was here - Redeem for a Firework

Expired Build A Boat For Treasure Codes

  • Happy Valentine's day - Redeem for free blocks
  • Be a big f00t print - Redeem for free blocks
  • fuzzy friend? - Redeem for free blocks
  • Lurking Legend - Redeem for free blocks
  • GGGOOOAAALLL - Redeem for a Football
  • Free gifts - Redeem for a bunch of free gifts!
  • 1B - Redeem for free blocks!
  • voted code - Redeem for free blocks!
  • Lurking Code - Redeem for 10x Balloons, 5x Super Fireworks, 1x Portal Block!
  • 1M Likes - Redeem for free blocks!
  • Big F00t Print - Redeem for 10x Balloons, 5x Cake, 25x Neon Blocks!
  • The Sasquatch? - Redeem for 10x Balloons, 5x Circle Fireworks, 1x Portal Block!
  • Fireworks - Redeem for free blocks!
  • 2M members - Redeem for free Blocks!
  • Hatched code - Redeem for Cookie Wheels!
  • Happy Easter - Redeem for Balloons, Candy, & Cake!
  • TTTRRREEEAAASSSUUURRREEE - Redeem for a Green Treasure Chest!
  • BBBOOOAAATTTSSS!!! - Redeem for 1x Boat Motor, 1x Car Seat, 4x Wood Block, 1x Cake!
  • The Yeti - Redeem for 25x Green Presents, 25x Neon Blocks!
  • Cold Feet - Redeem for 25x Green Presents, 25x Neon Blocks!
  • 500M Visits - Redeem for 4x Portal, 5x Cake, 25x Balloons, 20x Star Blocks!
  • Veterans Day - Redeem for 10x Banners, 10x Star Blocks, 10x Balloons, 5x Circle Fireworks, 5x Cluster Fireworks, 5x Super Fireworks!

How to Redeem Codes in Build a Boat For Treasure

To redeem codes in Build A Boat For Treasure, players will simply need to follow the steps outlined below. If a code does not work, they should be sure to check that it was entered correctly. Assuming it was, it may mean that the code has recently expired or has already been redeemed.

  1. Launch Build A Boat For Treasure
  2. Press the treasure icon located to the right of the screen
  3. Once the pop-up menu opens up, select the gear icon at the top right
  4. Paste the code into the field
  5. Press Redeem to collect the rewards

How to Play Build A Boat For Treasure

Build A Boat For Treasure is a game in which players need to make their own boat and sail as far as possible. First, players need to go to a special area where they can build and then select the tool in the second inventory slot. A menu of resources will appear there, and at first, there will be a few of them.

After the players have built their boat and placed a chair, they should sit in it and press launch. After that, players have to swim as far as possible through the obstacles. They need to be very careful because the boat can be destroyed. After reaching a certain distance, players will receive rewards and can then repeat the process.

Best Roblox Adventure Games Like Build A Boat For Treasure

There are many games similar to Build A Boat For Treasure. Below, players will find a list of the top five games like Build A Boat For Treasure :

  • Anime Adventures
  • Your Bizarre Adventure
  • Dragon Adventures
  • Boat Ride into a Better Life
  • Build a Boat or Die

About the Build A Boat For Treasure Developers

The team of developers, named Chillz Studios, who created Build A Boat For Treasure in 2016, has a lot of experience in creating games. Here is a list of other Roblox games from Chillz Studios that were popular before:

  • REVENGE OF THE SLIMES!!!
  • Mr. Cube The Boss Battle!
  • Marble Maker
  • Build and Burn
Roblox - Game
Released
September 1, 2006
